proven.claim: Claim: The summary line prints non‑negative counts for added, fixed, regressions, and removed.
proven.smt2:
; LINE: 306
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Semantic Correctness
; Claim: The summary line prints non‑negative counts for added, fixed, regressions, and removed.
;   ⇔ added ≥ 0 ∧ fixed ≥ 0 ∧ regressions ≥ 0 ∧ removed ≥ 0.
; PROVEN: we assert the code’s computation of those counts and then the negation.

; The code obtains the counts by filtering the `changes` array:
;   added      = changes.filter(c => c.type === "added").length
;   fixed      = … “fixed”
;   regressions= … “regressed”
;   removed    = … “removed”
; All lengths are naturally ≥ 0.

(declare-const added Int)
(declare-const fixed Int)
(declare-const regressions Int)
(declare-const removed Int)

; Implicit guarantees from array length:
(assert (>= added 0))
(assert (>= fixed 0))
(assert (>= regressions 0))
(assert (>= removed 0))

; Negation of the claim: at least one count is negative.
(assert (or (< added 0) (< fixed 0) (< regressions 0) (< removed 0)))

(check-sat)   ; expected: unsat
